debt_collection.name: Debt Collection / Default Notice
debt_collection.governing_law: Consumer Credit Act 1974, FCA CONC (Consumer Credit sourcebook) rules, Limitation Act 1980
key_rights: Right to request debt verification in writing — collector must pause collection
Statute of limitations: debt becomes unenforceable after 6 years (Limitation Act 1980)
Collectors cannot harass, use deceptive language, or threaten action they cannot take
Right to a free debt relief order or IVA if debts are unmanageable
Right to complain to Financial Ombudsman Service — free
Default Notice under Consumer Credit Act must give 14 days to remedy before any action
common_violations: Threatening court action on a statute-barred debt (over 6 years old)
Misrepresenting the legal status of the debt or the collector's identity
Not providing required default notice under Consumer Credit Act before action
Contacting at unreasonable times or excessive frequency (FCA CONC 7.9)
Claiming the debt is more than legally owed including unauthorized charges
debt_collection.forum: Financial Ombudsman Service or County Court
debt_collection.template_response: I write in response to your notice dated [DATE]. I request written verification of this debt, including the original creditor name, full account history, and proof of your authority to collect. I am aware of my rights under the FCA CONC rules and the Consumer Credit Act 1974. Please note that if this debt is statute-barred under the Limitation Act 1980, I do not acknowledge it. I reserve the right to complain to the Financial Ombudsman Service.

eviction_notice.name: Eviction Notice (Section 21 / Section 8)
eviction_notice.governing_law: Housing Act 1988, Deregulation Act 2015, Renters Reform Act
key_rights: Section 21 notice requires minimum 2 months written notice
Section 21 is invalid if landlord has not protected deposit in a government scheme
Section 21 invalid if landlord has not provided Energy Performance Certificate and How to Rent guide
Right to challenge retaliatory eviction in court
Cannot be evicted without a court order — bailiffs require a warrant of possession
common_violations: Section 21 notice served without deposit protected in government scheme
Section 21 notice without providing required How to Rent guide or EPC
Attempting to evict without a court order (illegal eviction — criminal offence)
Changing locks or cutting off utilities to force tenant out (criminal offence)
eviction_notice.template_response: I acknowledge your notice dated [DATE]. I wish to query whether the notice is valid. I request confirmation that my tenancy deposit has been protected in a government-approved scheme and that all required documents were provided. If the notice is not valid, I require you to withdraw it. I am aware that only a court can order my removal and that any illegal eviction is a criminal offence.

court_summons.name: County Court Claim / Legal Notice
court_summons.governing_law: Civil Procedure Rules 1998, County Courts Act 1984
key_rights: 14 days to respond to a County Court claim (or 28 days to file a defence)
Failure to respond results in a County Court Judgment (CCJ) affecting credit
Small claims track for disputes under £10,000 — no need for a solicitor
Right to free advice from Citizens Advice Bureau
Right to apply for set-aside if judgment was entered without proper service
common_violations: Claim issued in wrong court or wrong track
court_summons.template_response: I am in receipt of the County Court claim [Claim Number]. I intend to file my Acknowledgement of Service and Defence within the time allowed. I am disputing this claim in full and will file detailed grounds of defence. I request any further correspondence be directed to the address above.

employment_termination.name: Termination / Dismissal Letter
employment_termination.governing_law: Employment Rights Act 1996, ACAS Code of Practice on Disciplinary and Grievance Procedures
key_rights: Right to written reasons for dismissal after 2 years of employment
Right to appeal against dismissal as per ACAS Code
3 months minus one day to claim unfair dismissal at Employment Tribunal
Statutory redundancy pay: 0.5–1.5 weeks per year of service based on age
Minimum notice: 1 week per year of service (up to 12 weeks)
common_violations: Dismissal without following ACAS disciplinary procedure (renders it potentially unfair)
No right to be accompanied at disciplinary hearing by a colleague or union rep
Dismissal without required notice period or pay in lieu
Constructive dismissal — forced resignation due to employer's conduct
employment_termination.forum: ACAS Early Conciliation then Employment Tribunal
employment_termination.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the letter dated [DATE] informing me of my dismissal. I request written reasons for my dismissal as required under the Employment Rights Act 1996. I wish to appeal this decision and request the appeal procedure to be initiated. I reserve my rights to bring a claim at the Employment Tribunal if my appeal is unsuccessful.

consumer_notice.name: Consumer / Company Notice
consumer_notice.governing_law: Consumer Rights Act 2015, Consumer Contract Regulations 2013
key_rights: 30-day right to a full refund for faulty goods under Consumer Rights Act 2015
Up to 6 months — trader must prove goods were not faulty at delivery
14-day cooling off period for online and distance contracts
Right to complain to Trading Standards or the relevant Ombudsman
Citizens Advice consumer helpline: 0808 223 1133 (free)
common_violations: Refusing refund within 30-day statutory right to reject
Attempting to charge for repair of goods that were faulty on delivery
Misrepresenting goods or services at point of sale
consumer_notice.forum: Trading Standards, sector Ombudsman, or Small Claims Court
consumer_notice.template_response: I write to formally notify you of a complaint regarding [product/service] purchased on [date] for [amount]. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015, I am entitled to [refund/repair/replacement] as the goods/services are not as described and are not fit for purpose. I require resolution within 14 days. If unresolved, I will escalate to Trading Standards and the relevant Ombudsman.
